code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about artists (or related entities) into a fluent description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(value: str) -> str:
        """Strip surrounding quotes and whitespace."""
        return value.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced by the cleaned object string.
    pred_map = {
        "birthDate": "was born on {obj}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        # Prefer a natural phrasing that matches reference sentences.
        "genre": "performs {obj} music",
        "instrument": "plays the {obj}",
        "occupation": "is a {obj}",
        "nationality": "is from {obj}",
        # Use a generic “associated with” wording instead of “member of the band”.
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "is associated with {obj}",
        # Mention the record label explicitly.
        "recordLabel": "is signed to the record label {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started their career in {obj}",
        "activeYearsEndYear": "ended their career in {obj}",
        "deathDate": "died on {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "alternativeName": "is also known as {obj}",
        "stylisticOrigin": "has its stylistic origins in {obj}",
        "musicFusionGenre": "is fused with {obj}",
        "musicSubgenre": "is a subgenre of {obj}",
        "derivative": "has a derivative {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {obj}",
        # leader is handled specially to place the object as the subject.
        "leader": "leader is {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "country": "belongs to the country {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "is owned by {obj}",
        "bandMember": "has band member {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "anthem": "has anthem {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {obj}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "distributingCompany": "is distributed by {obj}",
        "training": "was trained at {obj}",
        "populationDensity": "has a population density of {obj}",
        "meaning": "means \"{obj}\"",
        "longName": "full name is \"{obj}\"",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies at {obj} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {obj}",
        "background": "is a {obj}",
    }

    # Group clauses by their subject to allow multiple subjects in the input.
    # First collect objects per subject and predicate to allow aggregation.
    subject_pred_objs: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj = t.subject
        pred = t.predicate
        obj = _clean(t.object)

        subject_pred_objs.setdefault(subj, {}).setdefault(pred, []).append(obj)

    # Build clauses, merging multiple objects for the same predicate.
    subject_clauses: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for subj, pred_dict in subject_pred_objs.items():
        # Extract and possibly merge background and genre for a more natural description
        bg_objs = pred_dict.pop("background", [])
        genre_objs = pred_dict.pop("genre", [])
        clauses: list[str] = []

        # Process background and genre together, enriching genre with any secondary information.
        if bg_objs and genre_objs:
            bg = bg_objs[0]
            g = genre_objs[0]
            # Build enriched genre description.
            enriched_genre = g
            # Look for additional info where the genre itself is a subject.
            extra = subject_pred_objs.get(g, {})
            # musicFusionGenre
            fusion_objs = extra.get("musicFusionGenre", [])
            if fusion_objs:
                fusion_str = ", ".join(fusion_objs[:-1]) + " and " + fusion_objs[-1] if len(fusion_objs) > 1 else fusion_objs[0]
                enriched_genre += f", fused with {fusion_str}"
            # derivative
            derivative_objs = extra.get("derivative", [])
            if derivative_objs:
                der_str = ", ".join(derivative_objs[:-1]) + " and " + derivative_objs[-1] if len(derivative_objs) > 1 else derivative_objs[0]
                enriched_genre += f", which has a derivative {der_str}"
            # musicSubgenre
            subgenre_objs = extra.get("musicSubgenre", [])
            if subgenre_objs:
                sub_str = ", ".join(subgenre_objs[:-1]) + " and " + subgenre_objs[-1] if len(subgenre_objs) > 1 else subgenre_objs[0]
                enriched_genre += f", a subgenre of {sub_str}"
            # stylisticOrigin
            origin_objs = extra.get("stylisticOrigin", [])
            if origin_objs:
                ori_str = ", ".join(origin_objs[:-1]) + " and " + origin_objs[-1] if len(origin_objs) > 1 else origin_objs[0]
                enriched_genre += f", with stylistic origins in {ori_str}"
            combined_clause = f"is a {bg} {enriched_genre} artist"
            clauses.append(combined_clause)
        else:
            # Process any leftover background or genre individually.
            if bg_objs:
                template = pred_map.get("background", "background {obj}")
                clauses.append(template.format(obj=bg_objs[0]))
            if genre_objs:
                genre_clauses = []
                for g in genre_objs:
                    enriched = g
                    extra = subject_pred_objs.get(g, {})
                    # Append any known secondary predicates.
                    fusion_objs = extra.get("musicFusionGenre", [])
                    if fusion_objs:
                        fusion_str = ", ".join(fusion_objs[:-1]) + " and " + fusion_objs[-1] if len(fusion_objs) > 1 else fusion_objs[0]
                        enriched += f", fused with {fusion_str}"
                    derivative_objs = extra.get("derivative", [])
                    if derivative_objs:
                        der_str = ", ".join(derivative_objs[:-1]) + " and " + derivative_objs[-1] if len(derivative_objs) > 1 else derivative_objs[0]
                        enriched += f", which has a derivative {der_str}"
                    subgenre_objs = extra.get("musicSubgenre", [])
                    if subgenre_objs:
                        sub_str = ", ".join(subgenre_objs[:-1]) + " and " + subgenre_objs[-1] if len(subgenre_objs) > 1 else subgenre_objs[0]
                        enriched += f", a subgenre of {sub_str}"
                    origin_objs = extra.get("stylisticOrigin", [])
                    if origin_objs:
                        ori_str = ", ".join(origin_objs[:-1]) + " and " + origin_objs[-1] if len(origin_objs) > 1 else origin_objs[0]
                        enriched += f", with stylistic origins in {ori_str}"
                    genre_clauses.append(f"musical genre is {enriched}")
                clauses.append(" and ".join(genre_clauses))

        # Process remaining predicates normally.
        for pred, objs in pred_dict.items():
            template = pred_map.get(pred, f"{pred} {{obj}}")
            if len(objs) == 1:
                obj_str = objs[0]
            else:
                obj_str = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + " and " + objs[-1]
            clause = template.format(obj=obj_str) if "{obj}" in template else template
            clauses.append(clause)

        subject_clauses[subj] = clauses

    sentences: list[str] = []
    for subj, clauses in subject_clauses.items():
        # Join clauses with commas and an 'and' before the last clause.
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            body = clauses[0]
        else:
            body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
        sentence = f"{subj} {body}."
        sentences.append(sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:])

    # Combine sentences for different subjects with a space.
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
